title: NOC 74203 – Automotive and heavy truck and equipment parts installers and servicers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 74203 professionals are the specialized technicians responsible for the repair and replacement of specific vehicle components. As a TEER 4 occupation, this group focuses on high-turnover parts such as mufflers, tires, brakes, and windshields for both passenger vehicles and heavy industrial trucks. In 2026, as Canada mandates the transition to Electric Vehicles (EV) and expands its Heavy-Duty Transport corridors, these installers are classified as Essential Maintenance Talent. For immigration, this role is a key target for the Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot and the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level4Major Group74 – Mail and message distribution, transport equipment operators and maintenanceSub-Major Group742 – Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related workersMinor Group7420 – Transport equipment operators, utility maintenance and related workersNOC Code74203

duties: Replacement & Installation: Replace and install specialized vehicle parts such as windshields, windows, mufflers, exhaust pipes, shock absorbers, and radiators.
Tire Service: Repair, mount, and balance tires for passenger vehicles and heavy industrial trucks; perform wheel alignments using computerized equipment.
System Testing: Inspect and test vehicle systems (brakes, exhaust, cooling) to detect malfunctions and ensure replacements are functioning correctly.
Maintenance: Perform minor tune-ups, oil changes, and lubrication; assist mechanics in more complex heavy equipment repairs.
ADAS Calibration: (Automotive Glass) Calibrate cameras and sensors integrated into windshields to ensure the vehicle's safety systems are accurate after glass replacement.
Reporting: Maintain detailed records of parts replaced and services performed; provide cost estimates to customers.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is the standard requirement.
Training: Several months of on-the-job training are typically provided by the employer or franchised service centers.
Education: Some specialized college courses in automotive glass repair or tire service may be required for senior roles.

Certifications:A valid driver’s license is almost always a mandatory requirement.Ozone Depletion Prevention (ODP) certificate may be required for those working with radiators and AC systems.Industry certifications (e.g., TIA for tire service) are highly valued in 2026.
A valid driver’s license is almost always a mandatory requirement.
Ozone Depletion Prevention (ODP) certificate may be required for those working with radiators and AC systems.
Industry certifications (e.g., TIA for tire service) are highly valued in 2026.
Physical strength for handling heavy tires and manual dexterity for precision glass fitting are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, specialized Heavy Truck Tire Servicers or Advanced Glass Technicians in urban centers often earn annual salaries between $58,000 and $78,000 inclusive of overtime.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that as vehicles become more complex, the need for professional installers to manage high-tech components (like acoustic glass and low-rolling-resistance tires for EVs) will increase. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), Retirements will create over 15,000 job openings across Canada through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for parts installers and servicers in 2026 is Good to Very Good. In Ontario and Alberta, the outlook is "Good" due to a growing national vehicle fleet and a 12% increase in heavy truck transport for the resource sector. According to 2026 Job Bank data, there is a persistent labor shortage for technicians specialized in ADAS (Advanced Driver Assistance Systems) calibration and Heavy Equipment Tire Service, particularly in Northern and Rural communities.
The "Smart Vehicle" Transition: Gain experience working with the latest automotive glass technology (HUDs) and specialized EV-grade high-performance tires.
Rapid Employment Entry: This role is an excellent entry point into the Canadian automotive sector, often requiring less formal schooling than a full Red Seal Mechanic license.
Diverse Career Path: Start by specializing in tires or glass and progress toward becoming an Automotive Service Technician (NOC 72410) through apprenticeship and on-the-job hours.

Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 4 role is a direct beneficiary of the 2026 federal and provincial pilots designed to retain skilled technical workers who have at least one year of Canadian experience.
pathways: New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A dedicated stream for 33,000 workers already in Canada, targeting those in TEER 4 and 5 roles with one year of experience.
Saskatchewan SINP (Hard-to-Fill Skills): A high-priority route for parts installers with a valid job offer in the province’s service sector.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Ideal for technicians currently employed in the province with a valid job offer from an automotive service provider.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion route for those with a job offer from a designated employer in an Atlantic province.
